effects.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

effects.text: {M} Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

effects.text: {C} Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the one-other-card discard cost and exact draw-until-six count
effects.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the this-turn Fighting-attacker and opponent-Active gates for +30 damage
effects.text: During this turn, attacks used by your {F} Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the this-turn opposing-Active-Pokémon-ex gate for +40 damage
effects.text: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon {ex} (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the three-other-card cost and four independently capped Trainer-subtype searches
effects.text:
You can use this card only if you discard 3 other cards from your hand.

Search your deck for an Item card, a Pokémon Tool card, a Supporter card, and a Stadium card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the exclusive choice between an own switch and a this-turn +30 modifier against opposing Active Pokémon ex/V
effects.text:
Choose 1:

• Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
• During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon {ex} and Active Pokémon {V} (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the Basic Energy search, reveal, and shuffle
effects.text: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

effects.text: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, the Retreat Cost of both Active Pokémon is {C} more.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits bottom-seven inspection, optional Pokémon selection/reveal, and rest-to-deck shuffle
effects.text: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits healing 70 from the damaged own Active
effects.text: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits up-to-five Basic Energy discard-to-deck movement and shuffle
effects.text: Shuffle up to 5 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits applying both Burned and Confused to the opponent's Active
effects.text: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the opponent-two-or-fewer-Prizes gate, any-own-Pokémon selector, ex-only attacker filter, and next-turn damage-and-effects prevention
effects.text:
You can use this card only if your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.

Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play. During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to that Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon {ex}.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits once-per-turn deck evolution through matching Stage 1 then optional matching Stage 2, plus normal Basic timing restrictions
effects.formula_question: all legal Basic→Stage1→optional Stage2 chains from deck
effects.text: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Stage 1 Pokémon that evolves from 1 of their Basic Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon was evolved in this way, that player may search their deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, that player shuffles their deck. (Players can’t evolve a Basic Pokémon during their first turn or a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.)

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits the more-Prizes-remaining gate and up-to-two Basic Energy discard attachment to one own Stage 2
effects.formula_question: legal iff own prizes > opponent prizes; attach 0–2 eligible cards to one Stage 2
effects.text:
You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.

Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits both-hand replacement and the exact fair-coin 5/3 versus 3/5 distribution
effects.text: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.

effects.change_from_current: the current encoder omits discarding the entire hand and three independently capped Pokémon/Supporter/Basic-Energy searches
effects.text: Discard your hand and search your deck for a Pokémon, a Supporter card, and a Basic Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

effects.text:
You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.

Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
